mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equities, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow quarter-over-quarter, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >0.5% month-over-month fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of cases during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive policy forces central banks to maintain higher rates for longer, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll additions signal robust economic activity and wage growth, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with PAYEMS growth exceeding 100k show negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >58% of cases, invalidating the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized monthly increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm broad-based economic strength, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 price adjustments over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal produces negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
